3660: ENTRY POINT — SEI, disable all interrupts/DMA, zero hardware regs, silence audio
36AF: Disable OS ROM: PORTB AND #$FE — game owns $C000-$FFFF as RAM
36BF: Set NMI vector $FFFA/B -> $91C1 (immediate VBI handler in RAM)
36C2: Set IRQ vector $FFFE/F -> $9100 (first IRQ handler in chain)
36CE: Clear framebuffer: $F800-$FFFF (8KB under OS ROM, self-modifying high byte)
36E4: PAL/NTSC detection via SKSTAT bit 3 — sets viewport width
370A: Init game state: music trigger, speed, FPS counter
371A: JSR $8ADA — setup buffer pointers for double-buffer rendering
3724: Wait for VCOUNT=$78 — sync to specific scanline before enabling display
3729: Set initial viewport colors: COLPF1=$0C, COLPF2=$04
3738: PRIOR=$21 — normal playfield mode + PMG priority 1
373D: CHBASE=$F8 — custom charset at $F800 (RAM under ROM)
374C: Player-missile setup: P0/P1 at $78 (crosshair center), double width
3772: P2 = $80 color, quad width — minimap overlay player
378E: CRITICAL: POKEY Timer 4 setup — AUDF4=$01, AUDCTL=$01 (15KHz base)
3798: Sync timer to scanline: WSYNC pair to align with HBLANK
379E: Read VCOUNT, wait for it to increment (2-scanline boundary)
37A9: Align to clean scanline pair start with double WSYNC
37B4: 10x NOP — precise delay for timer counter reset
37C5: STIMER=$01 — trigger all timer counters to reload NOW
37CA: CLI — interrupts enabled, timer chain begins firing
37D5: DMACTL=$3D — standard playfield DMA + PMG + normal width
37DA: MAIN LOOP — no frame sync; renders as fast as CPU allows (~24 FPS PAL)
37E7: RENDER FRAME — full pipeline: input → movement → raycast → draw → status
37EF: RAYCAST ALL COLUMNS — the hot path (~40% of frame budget)
37F2: Draw textured wall columns + sky/floor fill to framebuffer
37F5: Check CONSOL keys — flash COLBK if any pressed
3814: Flip display list pointer (double buffer swap)
383A: UPDATE MOVEMENT — apply turn accumulator and move player through map
3B4F: FLIP DISPLAY LIST — toggles JMP target at $9809 between $98 (buf 0) and $9A (buf 1)
3B5F: INC FRAME COUNTER — 16-bit counter at $10/$11, used for FPS calc
3B66: VBI HELPER — decrement FPS timer, every 100 frames: compute FPS and update display
3B83: DRAW HEX BYTE — convert X to 2 hex digits, write to status bar at position Y in $9B95
3BA5: Fade effect: decrement timer, then ramp COLBK up by $10 per step
3BBB: SETUP NMI VECTOR — write $91C1 to $FFFA/B (RAM under ROM)
3BC6: SETUP IRQ VECTOR — write $9100 to $FFFE/F (RAM under ROM)
3BD1: RENDER COLUMNS — iterates viewport columns ($71 to $72), raycasts + draws each
3BD5: Load column angle from lookup table
3BE9: Setup ray direction for first column
3C06: Compute perspective projection for this column
3C17: COLUMN LOOP — cast ray, store results, advance to next column
3C21: Store wall distance to $8F00,Y; texture to $8F20,Y
3C46: Advance column index, loop until viewport filled
3C54: Advance ray to next column angle
419D: SETUP RAY — compute ray direction from player angle + column offset, select quadrant
41BC: Determine ray quadrant (self-modifying: patches JSR target in cast_ray)
41E2: Setup X step direction based on quadrant
41F0: Setup Y step direction based on quadrant
41FA: ADVANCE RAY — step ray angle to next viewport column
4243: Boundary handling: wrap ray angle, reconfigure step directions
439F: CAST RAY — DDA (Digital Differential Analyzer) wall detection through map grid
43A7: Load precomputed ray step increments from tables at $4A00-$4C09
4402: DDA main loop: step through grid cells, test for wall hit
440A: Compare ray integer position against step boundary
441E: Step to next grid cell (fractional accumulator)
443F: Wall hit: check wall type, compute texture column from hit position
4475: Compute texture column offset (hit position >> 3 = nibble within wall)
447C: SELF-MODIFYING: patch BSP comparison addresses from ray position
4486: Call BSP traversal to determine visible wall face
448C: No wall hit: advance ray by full grid step, continue DDA
8DC6: BSP TRAVERSE — walk binary space partition tree (max depth 6) to find visible wall
8DC9: SELF-MODIFYING: patch CMP operands with current wall boundary addresses
8DDE: Start at root node (X=$1F), max 6 levels deep (Y=6)
8DE2: BSP inner loop: compare ray position against node split plane
8DE4: SELF-MODIFIED: CMP $CF00,X — compare against wall distance threshold
8DF6: SELF-MODIFIED: CMP $CF40,X — compare against wall height threshold
8E0F: Exact match: wall found, return node index
4C37: PERSPECTIVE PROJECT — compute screen column heights from ray distances
4C3B: SELF-MODIFYING: negate delta for subtraction, patch inline operands
4C5A: Loop over ray steps: compute wall/floor heights using $C000-$C6FF tables (RAM under ROM)
4DC8: Column clear/draw routine: writes to framebuffer at $D800+ (RAM under ROM)
8AAA: DRAW ALL COLUMNS — 2nd pass: iterate columns, draw texture + sky/floor
8AAE: Column loop: load per-column state from $8F00-$8FFF arrays
8AC9: Next column; loop until viewport filled
8AD7: JMP ($24) — dispatch via render pointer table (buffer-dependent)
8ADA: SETUP BUFFER PTRS 0 — configure ZP pointers for buffer 0 rendering
8B03: SETUP BUFFER PTRS 1 — configure ZP pointers for buffer 1 rendering
8B2C: SETUP BUFFER PTRS 2 — configure ZP pointers for alternate buffer mode
5200: DRAW TEXTURE COLUMN — renders one vertical strip of textured wall
5204: Compare wall heights to determine visible extent
5215: SELF-MODIFYING: patch JSR target at $5242 with texture routine address
5231: Write RTS ($60) to texture entry, call it, then write STA ($99) — trick for indirect texture dispatch
5242: SELF-MODIFIED JSR — jumps to per-texture column drawing routine
50BC: DRAW SKY/FLOOR — fill gradient above/below wall for one column
50CD: Compute gradient address from column position
50EE: BIT $12 — test buffer flag to select $D800 (buf 0) or $D820 (buf 1) offset
89E4: PLOT COLUMN — write one column of nibble pixels to framebuffer
89E9: Load texture base from lookup table at $9020,X
89EF: Load wall distance to compute column height
89F5: Get framebuffer address from height-to-address table at $8962/$89A3
8A0C: Near wall: shift texture color into high nibble, add gradient
8A1F: Far wall: different color mapping (5-bit shift)
8B55: PROCESS INPUT — read joystick bits, fire button, console keys
8B5C: Forward: move player along facing direction
8BAC: BIT $83 — FIRE button: toggle minimap display mode (0/1/2)
8BF1: BIT $85 — SELECT: cycle through map levels (1-5)
8C7F: SWAP BUFFERS — switch active buffer pointers for next frame rendering
9100: IRQ STAGE 0 — toggle PRIOR $E1 (16 hues) → $61 (16 luminances), chain to $9126
9104: Set PRIOR=$E1 — GTIA mode 3: nibble selects hue, COLBK sets luminance
9107: Acknowledge IRQ: IRQEN=0 then IRQEN=$04 (re-enable Timer 4)
9113: Self-modify: set $FFFE=$26 → next IRQ dispatches to $9126
9116: LDA VCOUNT → STA COLPM3: raster bar effect on player 3
9120: Set PRIOR=$61 — GTIA mode 1: nibble selects luminance, COLBK sets hue
9126: IRQ STAGE 1 — same PRIOR toggle, 4 NOPs for timing, chain to $914A
914A: IRQ STAGE 2 — same PRIOR toggle + VCOUNT raster bar, chain to $9170
9170: IRQ STAGE 3 — PRIOR toggle + loop controller: DEC $04, if >0 loop to $9100
9183: Counter >0: loop back (set $FFFE=$00 → $9100), 2 NOPs for timing
91A3: IRQ BOTTOM — viewport complete: PRIOR=$21 (normal mode), set text colors
91A5: PRIOR=$21 — exit GTIA mode, standard 4-color playfield for status bar
91AC: Disable Timer 4 IRQ (no more PRIOR switching needed this frame)
91B4: Set text colors: COLPF1=$0E (white), COLPF2=$34 (dark blue)
91C1: NMI IMMEDIATE VBI — enable Timer 4 IRQ, reset scanline counter, chain to deferred
91C3: IRQEN=$04 — enable Timer 4 underflow IRQ for new frame
91C8: $04=$10 — reset scanline counter to 16 (16 iterations × 8 scanlines = 128)
91CC: Self-modify NMI: $FFFA=$D4 → next NMI goes to deferred handler $91D4
91D4: NMI DEFERRED VBI — set viewport colors, music player, FPS timing
91E4: INC $13 — VBI sub-counter; INC $14 — VBI frame counter
91E6: JSR vbi_helper — FPS calculation and background fade
91EB: Music dispatch: $89=trigger, $8B=state; routes to RMT player or silence
9222: Restore NMI: $FFFA=$C1 → next NMI goes back to immediate handler $91C1
3303: RMT PLAYER — Raster Music Tracker main entry, dispatches play/init/stop
3309: RMT SILENCE — mute all channels, set AUDCTL=$01
334F: RMT STOP — zero all AUDF/AUDC registers, return
336B: RMT INIT CHANNELS — parse instrument headers, fill channel state at $2FE0-$3000
33BB: RMT FRAME TICK — advance all 4 channels one step, update notes/envelopes
3632: UPDATE SOUND — write AUDF1-3 and AUDC1-3 from music state buffers $3048-$304E
9800: DISPLAY LIST 0 — 128 ANTIC mode F lines + blank + 2 text lines, LMS base $D800
9A00: DISPLAY LIST 1 — alternate buffer, LMS base $D820 (32-byte offset for interleave)
8E12: BSP NODE DATA — wall type, texture, and geometry for BSP tree nodes
9240: BSP CHILD POINTERS — left/right child indices for BSP tree traversal
C000: SIN TABLE — 256-entry sine lookup (unsigned 8-bit), copied by INIT
C400: COS/RECIPROCAL TABLE — cosine and reciprocal values for perspective
CF00: WALL DISTANCE TABLE — BSP comparison thresholds (32 entries)
CF40: WALL HEIGHT TABLE — height-for-distance lookup (32 entries)
F800: CUSTOM CHARSET — 128 characters at CHBASE $F8, copied by INIT
8F00: COL DISTANCE ARRAY — per-column wall distance (32 entries), indexed by column
8F20: COL TEXTURE ARRAY — per-column texture ID (32 entries)
8F40: COL TEXCOL ARRAY — per-column texture column offset (32 entries)
8F60: COL HEIGHT BUF0 — per-column pixel height for buffer 0
8F80: COL SKY BUF0 — per-column sky height for buffer 0
8FA0: COL HEIGHT BUF1 — per-column pixel height for buffer 1
8FC0: COL SKY BUF1 — per-column sky height for buffer 1
4000: INIT ROUTINE — runs during XEX loading, copies tables to RAM under OS ROM, then returns
4009: PORTB AND #$FE — disable OS ROM, expose RAM at $C000-$FFFF
4015: PAL detection: read GTIA $D014, check bit 1-3
